commands.review_summary:
The PR changes autogenerated `message(action=send)` idempotency keys from transient tool-call ids to run-scoped delivery fingerprints, with retry, successful-identical-send, parallel-send, and nested-payload regression tests.

PR surface: Source +96, Tests +135. Total +231 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main constructs keys from transient tool-call ids, the PR body includes an A/B failing regression on main, and the attached Mantis Telegram Desktop proof shows the baseline retry posting twice while the candidate keeps one message.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the delivery semantics and normal merge checks, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(message-tool): stabilize send idempotency keys
target.body:
## Summary

Stabilizes autogenerated `message(action=send)` idempotency keys so an exact caller retry after a gateway/client timeout reuses the same key instead of looking like a fresh send.

The duplicate-send path observed in Telegram was:

1. `message(action=send)` starts a gateway `message.action` request.
2. The local caller times out at 30s while the gateway-side send may still complete.
3. The caller/model retries with a new transient tool-call id.
4. The old autogenerated idempotency key included that transient tool-call id, so the retry bypassed gateway in-flight/dedupe and could send again.

This keeps explicit `idempotencyKey` untouched. For autogenerated keys, it now hashes canonical delivery params under the run id, ignoring non-delivery controls such as `timeoutMs`, `gatewayUrl`, `gatewayToken`, and any explicit `idempotencyKey` value.

## A/B Evidence

Baseline A, current `main` (`f5ccfb73193eedf8735f1ef438a07f937ec18ae4`) plus only the new regression test:

```text
Test Files  1 failed (1)
Tests       1 failed | 94 passed (95)

FAIL uses stable delivery params so exact retries reuse the idempotency key
Expected: "run-message-tool:message-tool:message_222_1"
Received: "run-message-tool:message-tool:message_111_1"
```

This shows the old behavior: same send params, different tool-call ids, different idempotency keys.

Variant B, patched branch:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       95 passed (95)
```

Command used for the passing run:

```bash
corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts
```

`git diff --check` is clean.

## Related Reports

Related, but broader than this PR:

- #87068: Telegram channel duplicate outbound sends per turn.
- #51628: Telegram delivery queue recovery can replay old `sendMessage` replies and duplicate delivery-mirror transcript entries.
- #37446: Sub-agent timeout recovery creates duplicate API posts (idempotency gap).

This PR addresses the message-tool autogenerated idempotency-key gap seen when the visible send path times out but may still complete. It does not claim to fully solve legacy Telegram delivery queue replay/recovery policy from #51628.

## #70543 Check

I checked #70543 (`feat(exec): add normalized auto mode`). It does not appear to be the direct cause of this bug:

- #70543 is an exec policy/approval-mode PR.
- It did not touch `src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts` or `src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`.
- Related duplicate Telegram/replay reports existed independently, including #51628 from March.

## Test Plan

```bash
corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts
git diff --check
```
target.author: jesse-merhi
